honkaiNexusAnimaReleaseDate.title: Honkai Nexus Anima Release Date, Test Status, and Platforms
honkaiNexusAnimaReleaseDate.intro: This section gives players the practical release information they are searching for first: whether the game is out, what platforms are supported, how the current closed test works, and where Honkai: Nexus Anima fits in the wider Honkai lineup.

rows.details: A full public launch date has not been listed yet. The latest confirmed milestone is the Evolution Test beginning July 9, 2026 (UTC+8).
Honkai: Nexus Anima is in a limited testing stage. The Evolution Test is a free closed beta with account data wiped after the test.
The Evolution Test is available across PC and mobile devices, including iOS and Android.
Official and press materials position the game for PC and mobile play. Store download pages for the public launch are not the main release channel yet.
Players collect Anima companions, build bonds with them, explore the overworld, and use Anima abilities in Nexus Battles.
Honkai: Nexus Anima is a new installment in the Honkai series with a different creature-collection and strategy focus from Honkai Impact 3rd and Honkai: Star Rail.

honkaiNexusAnimaEvolutionTest.title: How the Honkai Nexus Anima Evolution Test Works
honkaiNexusAnimaEvolutionTest.intro: Before full release, most player intent is about beta access instead of codes or rerolling. This guide explains the official Evolution Test flow, including the recruitment period, tester selection, platforms, and data wipe rules.

steps.title: Use the official website
steps.description: Go to the official Honkai: Nexus Anima website and sign in with a HoYoverse account before using any registration or test access feature.

steps.title: Complete pre-registration
steps.description: Pre-registration connects the HoYoverse account to the test access process and official follow-up notices.

steps.title: Submit the recruitment questionnaire
steps.description: Players who complete the questionnaire enter the selection pool for the Evolution Test. Contact details should be filled in carefully because test notices are tied to the submitted information.

steps.title: Check the recruitment window
steps.description: Evolution Test recruitment runs from June 22, 2026 12:00 to July 3, 2026 23:59 (UTC+8).

steps.title: Watch for tester notification
steps.description: Tester access notifications begin from July 8, 2026 (UTC+8). Players can follow official channels and use the official site access check when available.

steps.title: Play on supported test platforms
steps.description: The Evolution Test begins July 9, 2026 (UTC+8) and supports PC, iOS, and Android. The test is free, limited, closed, and uses a data wipe.

honkaiNexusAnimaBeginnerGuide.title: Honkai Nexus Anima Beginner Guide: How to Start Playing
honkaiNexusAnimaBeginnerGuide.intro: This beginner guide explains the core flow players see in official gameplay: start as a plane-hopping traveler, explore new areas, collect Anima companions, strengthen bonds, and use Anima abilities in missions and Nexus Battles.

steps.title: Follow the main journey first
steps.description: The player takes the role of a plane-hopping traveler whose story begins in a quiet town before expanding into new realms. Main missions introduce the world, characters, and basic systems in order.
steps.tip: Use story progress to unlock systems before chasing advanced team building.

steps.title: Learn what Nexus and Anima mean
steps.description: Nexus represents bonds between paired concepts such as Love and Hate or Light and Dark. When those bonds are shattered, their remnant power appears as Anima.
steps.tip: Treat Anima as both companions and gameplay tools, not just collectible creatures.

steps.title: Collect Anima companions
steps.description: Anima are the central companions of the game. They have distinct personalities and are used across exploration and combat.
steps.tip: Build a flexible collection instead of focusing on only one favorite Anima early.

steps.title: Build bonds with Anima
steps.description: Bonding with Anima unlocks abilities based on the concepts they embody. These abilities can change how players fight, explore, and solve encounters.
steps.tip: Prioritize Anima that help in both overworld exploration and Nexus Battles.

steps.title: Understand Nexus Battles
steps.description: In Nexus Battles, Anima companions stand beside the player and support strategic combat. Combining Anima abilities is part of building stronger battle tactics.
steps.tip: Pay attention to ability combinations rather than raw power alone.

steps.title: Use official videos to learn systems
steps.description: The official gameplay showcase and teaser videos show exploration, Anima companions, customization, and combat flow before the full public release.
steps.tip: Watch the gameplay showcase before entering the test so the UI and combat flow feel familiar.

honkaiNexusAnimaAnimaList.title: Honkai Nexus Anima List: Revealed Anima and Collection Basics
honkaiNexusAnimaAnimaList.intro: This collection guide highlights officially shown or publicly introduced Anima and explains what players can understand from current trailers and DevTalk posts: Anima have distinct visual themes, are tied to the game's bond system, and can influence both combat and overworld exploration.

cards.name: Umpurrella
cards.description: A cat-like umbrella companion featured in the official Nexusbound teaser Watersong: A Sea of Flowers. Presented as a bond-focused companion tied to the wider Anima collection system.

cards.name: Seroplumanth
cards.description: An Anima born from withered plum branches and associated with swirling snow and plum blossoms. Shows how Anima designs connect creature identity with concept-driven themes.

cards.name: Cublade
cards.description: A small blade-themed creature shown in official event and test-related promotional materials, including merchandise rewards. A cute-but-combat-ready example of the roster.

cards.name: Bobabirb
cards.description: A bird-like Anima with a food and drink motif, mentioned among the newer Anima shown around the test period. Fits the playful creature-collector tone of the game.

cards.name: Foalhardy
cards.description: A foal-like companion design mentioned among the newer Anima revealed around the closed beta period. Helps fill the roster with animal-inspired variety.

cards.name: Scorscythe
cards.description: A scorpion and scythe-inspired design mentioned as one of the latest Anima revealed around the test period. Represents the sharper battle-oriented side of the roster.

cards.name: Puddlipup
cards.description: A pudding-like puppy design highlighted in early media coverage as one of the whimsical creature designs that define the game's style and collectible identity.

cards.name: Collection System
cards.description: Players collect Anima, form bonds, and unlock abilities connected to the concepts each Anima embodies. Anima affect both Nexus Battles and exploration, so collection progress is tied directly to gameplay.

honkaiNexusAnimaTierList.title: Honkai Nexus Anima Role Priority Grid
honkaiNexusAnimaTierList.intro: Honkai: Nexus Anima teams are built around Anima companions, Aspect themes, and auto-battler-style Nexus Battles. Use this grid to understand which revealed Anima fit frontline pressure, backline utility, bond-focused collection, and evolution learning.

tiers.summary: Best picks to explain basic team structure and Nexus Battle positioning.

entries.name: Fyrklor
entries.description: A greatsword-wielding Anima built around strength and protection.
entries.teamFit: Place it forward to create space for ranged, utility, or bond-focused Anima behind it.

entries.name: Scorscythe
entries.description: A bladed-tail Anima tied to the War Aspect.
entries.teamFit: Use it in aggressive setups that need a direct battle threat.

tiers.summary: Anima that work well for explaining companion behavior, visual identity, and role variety.

entries.name: Umpurrella
entries.description: A parasol-like Anima featured in the Watersong teaser and city branding previews.
entries.teamFit: Use as a recognizable Anima card for players learning companion identity and themed utility.

entries.name: Camcorgi
entries.description: A dog-like Anima associated with capturing life through its lens.
entries.teamFit: Use it for exploration, memory, photo, and companion-focused team notes.

tiers.summary: Strong picks for beginner collection cards because their themes are easy to understand.

entries.name: Vulberry
entries.description: A cream-and-strawberry-themed Anima with a Chromatic variant shown in official updates.
entries.teamFit: Use it for collection, personality, and rare-variant explanations.

entries.name: Hoggit
entries.description: A piglet Anima with an apple-pie scent and a relaxed, food-loving personality.
entries.teamFit: Use in beginner cards that explain Anima personality and bond appeal.

entries.name: Dragamour
entries.description: A Love Aspect Anima tied to legends and emotional encounters.
entries.teamFit: Use it in lore, bond, and companion-relationship team examples.

tiers.summary: Best picks for explaining evolution, growth, and long-term Anima development.

entries.name: Inkifoal Line
entries.description: A three-stage Anima evolution line (Inkifoal to Inkicorn to Atragasus) shown in Evolution Test updates.
entries.teamFit: Use it to teach players that Anima growth can change form and strengthen identity over time.

entries.name: Moneytree
entries.description: A Prosperity Aspect Anima tied to wealth and growth imagery.
entries.teamFit: Use it in resource, progression, and collection-themed UI cards.

honkaiNexusAnimaBattleSystem.title: Honkai Nexus Anima Battle System and Strategy
honkaiNexusAnimaBattleSystem.intro: Honkai: Nexus Anima uses creature-collecting adventure strategy gameplay with auto-battler-style combat. Players collect and train Anima, bring them into Nexus Battles, and use Aspect-based team planning to create different tactical setups.

faqs.question: What type of battle system does Honkai Nexus Anima use?
faqs.answer: Honkai: Nexus Anima uses adventure strategy combat built around Anima companions. Official gameplay footage shows arena-style battles where Anima fight semi-autonomously, making team setup and role balance more important than manual turn-by-turn commands.
keyPoints: Team planning before and during combat

faqs.question: How should beginners think about Honkai Nexus Anima team roles?
faqs.answer: Start with a simple team structure: one frontline pressure Anima, one damage or utility Anima, and one bond or support-themed Anima. This makes each slot easy to understand while leaving room for Aspect synergy as more Anima are revealed.
keyPoints: Utility Anima add control, support, or exploration value
Aspect identity helps define team style

faqs.question: What makes Aspect synergy important in Honkai Nexus Anima?
faqs.answer: Nexus refers to bonds between paired Aspects such as Love and Hate, Light and Dark, and Reality and Illusion. Anima carry remnant Aspect power, so team building should group companions by role, theme, and tactical use rather than appearance alone.
keyPoints: Pair role coverage with Aspect coverage
Use evolution lines to plan long-term growth

faqs.question: How does exploration connect to Honkai Nexus Anima combat?
faqs.answer: Anima are not only battle units; they also travel with the player across towns, planes, and challenge areas. Evolution, naming, photo moments, and Chromatic Anima collection all support the wider loop of bonding with companions before taking them into Nexus Battles.
keyPoints: Bring bonded Anima into Nexus Battles

faqs.question: What is the simplest early strategy in Honkai Nexus Anima?
faqs.answer: Build around one clear anchor Anima, then add partners that cover its weakness. A frontline Strength-style Anima works best when paired with safer backline or utility companions, while food-themed or bond-themed Anima are better used to explain collection and personality goals.
keyPoints: Do not stack only one role
Use simple placement logic before advanced synergy

honkaiNexusAnimaCharacters.title: Honkai Nexus Anima Characters and Story Guide
honkaiNexusAnimaCharacters.intro: The story follows a plane-hopping traveler who escapes captivity and begins a journey from Iia, a quiet town in the Reality Plane. As the player explores different Planes, they bond with Anima, uncover hidden truths, and meet characters connected to Nexus Hall, the True Nexus Building, and major factions in Iia.

cards.name: The Traveler
cards.description: The player character is a plane-hopping traveler who escaped captivity and begins the Evolution Test journey from Iia in the Reality Plane.
cards.hook: Their past is tied to the truths hidden across the world and the broken bonds of the Nexus.

cards.name: Nanafey
cards.description: Nanafey appears in the Character Teaser: The Wonder Girl Next Door and is one of the named characters crossing paths with the traveler during the Evolution Test.
cards.hook: Her teaser line focuses on curiosity about the world beyond town, making her a strong early story card.

cards.name: Bai Mei
cards.description: Bai Mei is listed among the new characters who meet the traveler as the Evolution Test expands the story and exploration path.
cards.hook: Use Bai Mei as a character card for new story encounters and faction-connected updates.

cards.name: Olympia
cards.description: Olympia is tied to the True Nexus Syndicate and appears as an Acting Director figure in Iia story updates.
cards.hook: Her role gives the story a sharper faction conflict around how humans and Anima should relate.

cards.name: Herocrates
cards.description: Herocrates is connected to the Nexus Association, a group focused on coexistence with Anima and rescue activity.
cards.hook: Use this card to explain Nexus Hall and the more peaceful side of Anima-human relations.

cards.name: Fleming Collective
cards.description: The Fleming Collective is a commercial faction connected to Fleming Tower and the city’s business side.
cards.hook: This faction adds corporate power, city landmarks, and political texture to Iia.

cards.name: True Nexus Syndicate
cards.description: The True Nexus Syndicate is presented as a more forceful faction that believes Nexus Anima should work for humans.
cards.hook: Use this faction card for conflict, authority, and underworld-style story hooks.

cards.name: Iia
cards.description: Iia is the starting town in the Reality Plane, with the New Quarter, commercial spaces, architectural landmarks, Nexus Hall, and the True Nexus Building.
cards.hook: Iia works as the homepage’s main location card because it connects exploration, factions, characters, and Anima life.

honkaiNexusAnimaLatestNews.title: Honkai Nexus Anima Latest News and Trailers
honkaiNexusAnimaLatestNews.intro: Honkai: Nexus Anima updates are published through the official website, HoYoLAB, X, and YouTube. This feed collects major official beats including Evolution Test recruitment, gameplay showcases, character teasers, Anidex posts, faction reveals, and DevTalk development updates.

items.title: Nanafey - The Wonder Girl Next Door
items.summary: The Nanafey character teaser launched as the Evolution Test began, giving players a focused look at one of the newly highlighted story characters.

items.title: Evolution Test Begins
items.summary: The Evolution Test begins across PC, iOS, and Android as a limited free closed beta with account data wiped after the test.

items.title: Vulberry, Inkifoal Evolution, and Iia Factions
items.summary: Official updates introduced Vulberry, highlighted the Inkifoal evolution line, and outlined factions including the Fleming Collective, Nexus Association, and True Nexus Syndicate.

items.title: Evolution Test Gameplay Showcase
items.summary: The gameplay showcase presents the expanded Evolution Test version, including Nexus Battle footage, exploration, Anima companions, and new gameplay systems.

items.title: Evolution Teaser - Until We Meet
items.summary: The Evolution teaser sets up the wider adventure, Anima bonds, and the player's journey across planes before the July Evolution Test.

items.title: Phase II - Environments and New Anima
items.summary: The DevTalk introduced Fyrklor and Hoggit, showed environment improvements for Iia, and previewed a new skyscraper district connected to Umpurrella.

items.title: Watersong: A Sea of Flowers
items.summary: The Umpurrella-focused teaser showed a softer Anima companion story and helped establish the game's bond-centered visual tone.

items.title: Official Social Media Overview
items.summary: HoYoLAB collected the official social channels for players who want the newest game updates, videos, benefits, and announcements.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Honkai Nexus Anima

questions.question: What is Honkai Nexus Anima?
questions.answer: Honkai Nexus Anima is HoYoverse's creature-collector adventure strategy game in the Honkai series. Players collect Anima companions, build bonds, explore the Planes, and use Anima abilities in Nexus Battles.

questions.question: When does the Honkai Nexus Anima Evolution Test run?
questions.answer: The Honkai Nexus Anima Evolution Test runs from July 9 to July 27, 2026 (UTC+8) as a free closed beta on PC, iOS, and Android, with account data wiped after the test ends.

questions.question: What platforms is Honkai Nexus Anima available on?
questions.answer: During the Evolution Test, Honkai Nexus Anima is available on PC, iOS, and Android. The full release is also focused on PC and mobile platforms.

questions.question: What are Anima in Honkai Nexus Anima?
questions.answer: Anima are the collectible companions at the center of Honkai Nexus Anima. Each Anima carries remnant power from a Nexus bond between paired Aspects and can be used in both exploration and Nexus Battles.

questions.question: How do I get Honkai Nexus Anima beta access?
questions.answer: To access the Honkai Nexus Anima Evolution Test, sign in on the official website with a HoYoverse account, complete pre-registration, and submit the recruitment questionnaire during the selection window.

questions.question: Is Honkai Nexus Anima free to play?
questions.answer: The Honkai Nexus Anima Evolution Test is a free closed beta. Full release pricing and monetization have not been announced yet.
